claim: All four skills use identical phase transition markers: '========== PHASE N: [NAME] =========='
All four skills share three unified critical rules: write-to-disk-first, state-file-on-every-phase, and conversational/non-clinical output

repo-analysis is the structural anchor of the family — at v4.2 with 22 SKILL.md sections and 18 REFERENCE.md sections, it is the oldest and most evolved skill. website-analysis explicitly states it 'mirrors /repo-analysis architecture'
The SKILL:REFERENCE ratio for analysis skills is 1:3.9 to 1:8.2; for synthesis skills it is 1:1.1 to 1:1.8 — this is a philosophy difference, not just a size difference

repo-synthesis has an incomplete SKILL/REFERENCE split: Guard Rails and Compaction Resilience moved to REFERENCE.md during the v1.2 skill audit, but Phase 2 in SKILL.md still contains inline heuristics
website-analysis is the only skill with a Decision Coverage Map appendix in REFERENCE.md — maps all 36 design decisions to their implementation location
Both analysis skills have an interactive gate after Phase 0 Quick Scan. Synthesis skills have no equivalent tier-selection gate — they proceed once validated
repo-synthesis is the only skill with an explicit Warm-Up pre-phase that presents scope estimate (repo count, candidate count, effort estimate, prior feedback) before work begins
repo-synthesis Phase 2.5 Verification Pass (T20 tally: confirmed/corrected/extended/new) has no equivalent in website-synthesis, repo-analysis, or website-analysis

Self-audit asymmetry: website-analysis (9-dimension MUST) and repo-synthesis (6-dimension MUST Phase 3) have formal self-audits; repo-analysis has only an artifact checklist; website-synthesis has only artifact existence check — the weakest
repo-analysis retro is SHOULD. repo-synthesis retro is MUST, persisted to state.process_feedback, and replayed in next run's Warm-Up — the only closed cross-session feedback loop in the family
Both analysis skills spawn agents; both synthesis skills do not. This is a clean architectural boundary — synthesis skills 'read, don't re-analyze'

Both analysis skills share an identical 5-step agent failure protocol, including the Windows 0-byte output bug workaround, suggesting deliberate cross-skill coordination referencing CLAUDE.md guardrail #15
Both analysis skills share a hard cap of 4 concurrent agents with wave staging for larger pools; website-analysis explicitly cross-references repo-analysis as its precedent
repo-analysis uses 7 named custom agents; website-analysis uses anonymous spawned agents with only code-reviewer as a named custom agent (for the self-audit post-analysis)
website-analysis spawns agents 'if content is substantial' with no quantitative threshold; repo-analysis has an explicit 20-file cutoff for inline vs agent processing
The agent_budget.allocated field in repo-analysis state schema is in tension with MEMORY.md feedback_no_agent_budgets.md ('allocation formula is a planning floor, not a spending cap')
All four skills share the same 4-band categorical scale (Critical/Needs Work/Healthy/Excellent) and 'Bands over numbers' display rule
Both analysis skills implement a critical floor metric — minimum dimension score displayed alongside overall score to prevent a high average masking a catastrophic failure on one dimension
website-analysis uses 13 value axes on a 1-5 scale with named rubric levels for all 13; repo-analysis has only 5 Knowledge dimensions with no equivalent rubric depth

repo-analysis is the only skill with dual-lens scoring: Adoption (Security 25%, Reliability 20%...) and Creator (Knowledge 35%...) — both computed on every analysis, primary auto-selected by repo type

website-synthesis has a formal convergence scoring formula with T1-T4 source tier weights (T1=3x, T4=0.5x, 6:1 ratio) and confidence thresholds (HIGH>=6.0, MEDIUM 3.0-5.9, LOW 1.5-2.9). repo-synthesis has none of this.

Fit scoring thresholds are perfectly shared across all four skills: active-sprint (personal_fit>=60), park-for-later, evergreen, not-relevant. This is a deliberate shared design noted in website-analysis REFERENCE.md

The repo-analysis REFERENCE.md v2.0 schema (single extraction_candidates[] with objective_score/personal_fit_score) does not match v4.2 runtime artifacts (4 typed arrays with novelty/effort/relevance). REFERENCE.md was not updated.
findings.jsonl schema drift: repo-analysis REFERENCE.md specifies 'detail' field; actual runtime files use 'description'. schema_version field is absent from actual files.

extraction-journal.jsonl has incompatible paths: website-analysis places it at .research/ (root); repo-analysis places it at .research/repo-analysis/. reading-chain.jsonl and EXTRACTIONS.md have the same conflict.

repo-synthesis synthesis.json uses 'version' key at top level; website-synthesis uses 'schema_version'. Both are documented intentionally — but they break forward-compatibility for a future cross-type synthesizer.
repo-synthesis output lives at .research/repo-analysis/SYNTHESIS.md (uppercase, root). website-synthesis output lives at .research/website-analysis/synthesis/synthesis.md (lowercase, subdirectory).
repo-analysis REFERENCE.md was not updated after v4.2 — still at v4.0. It is the only skill where SKILL.md and REFERENCE.md have different version numbers.

website-synthesis was designed after repo-synthesis and explicitly described as a 'companion' — yet paradoxically has more analytical rigor in convergence scoring and theme extraction. repo-synthesis has more operational robustness.

website-synthesis theme extraction uses 8-12 thematic questions fanned out across sites with 0-3 per-site scoring and a thematic saturation stopping rule. repo-synthesis uses qualitative prose reading with no structured method.

Both synthesis skills prohibit autonomous contradiction resolution. repo-synthesis makes this a hard MUST with a named output type (Contrarian Signal section); website-synthesis expresses the same intent more softly.
Both synthesis skills use 3+ instances as the minimum for synthesis and offer --min-N override. Only repo-synthesis documents the 2-instance edge case: 'comparison, not synthesis'.

repo-synthesis unique outputs: Mental Model Evolution (chronological perspective tracking), Reading Chain (ordered study sequence from relationship graph), Ecosystem Gap Analysis, Fit Portfolio refresh against current SESSION_CONTEXT.md

website-synthesis unique outputs: 4 synthesis paradigms (thematic/narrative/matrix/meta-pattern), formal convergence scoring, Knowledge Portfolio with convergence_boost flag, independence verification for convergence

Depth flag syntax is inconsistent between the two analysis skills: website-analysis uses --standard/--deep (bare flags); repo-analysis uses --depth=standard/--depth=deep (keyed flags). Not interchangeable.

repo-synthesis has the richest within-phase progress reporting: output counter '--- Output N of 6: [Name] ---', warm-up scope brief, T20 tally after verification. Other three skills show only phase-level markers.
All four skills present Creator View before Engineer View as a fixed ordering. There is no user flag to select 'engineer view only' or skip Creator View.

repo-synthesis delegation protocol ('you decide' → skill selects highest-impact action, records rationale as delegated-action in state) is unique in the family. Also unique: pause command saves state and exits gracefully mid-run.
Critical rules count: repo-analysis (10), website-analysis (8), website-synthesis (8), repo-synthesis (5). repo-synthesis has the fewest despite being a complex multi-output skill.

'No silent skips' is an explicit Critical Rule in website-analysis (Rule 6) and repo-analysis (Rule 4) but is absent from the Critical Rules sections of website-synthesis and repo-synthesis — despite the behavior existing.

website-analysis is the only skill with explicit Windows MAX_PATH algorithm for slug generation (80-char limit, SHA-256 suffix on collision/truncation). repo-analysis sidesteps it by cloning to /tmp/.
Anchoring prevention (analyzing all sites independently BEFORE synthesis begins) is an explicit guard in website-synthesis. repo-synthesis has no equivalent guard.

repo-analysis is the only skill with a home repo guard (redirect to /audit-comprehensive for sonash-v0), GitHub rate limit check (abort if remaining < 200), and TDMS anti-pollution rule (opt-in only via routing menu).
repo-analysis has the most formal dual-lens system: Adoption Lens and Creator Lens both computed on every analysis, primary auto-selected by repo type, both displayed with primary marked [PRIMARY]

website-analysis uses a single Creator Verdict (not a true dual-lens). The Engineer View contributes 20% to the composite score but produces no independent verdict. Calling it 'dual-lens' in SKILL.md is misleading.

'Extract' verdict appears in both Adoption lens (Extract: don't adopt whole, cherry-pick parts) and Creator lens (Extract: low learning priority, cherry-pick insights) — same word, different decision context, potential ambiguity.

Creator View section ordering diverges: website-analysis leads with 'What's Relevant To Your Work' (Section 1); repo-analysis leads with 'What This Repo Understands' (Section 1). Creates comparison confusion across skill types.
All four skills load the same 5 home context sources before Creator View: SESSION_CONTEXT.md, ROADMAP.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/ listing, MEMORY.md
TDMS integration exists only in repo-analysis. website-analysis, website-synthesis, and repo-synthesis have no TDMS routing option or field mapping.
Invocation tracking is inconsistent: website-analysis MUST (write-invocation.ts), website-synthesis explicit bash command, repo-analysis no call documented, repo-synthesis not documented.
research-index.jsonl (written by repo-analysis every run, read at session-begin) makes repo-analysis the only session-level integrated skill in the family

Cross-type synthesis schema is partially aligned by design: both synthesis.json files share schema_version, synthesized_at, paradigm_output, and signals structure intentionally for forward compatibility
repo-synthesis is the only synthesis skill that escalates to /deep-research (in 'Explore a theme' follow-up action). website-synthesis does not document this escalation path.
No skill creates git branches, commits, or PRs. All artifact production is filesystem only. repo-analysis clones to /tmp/ for analysis only (auto-cleanup).
website-analysis has a unique HARD_BLOCK compliance system (robots.txt, X-Robots-Tag, llms.txt) with a WARN tier requiring explicit user acknowledgment. No equivalent in the other three skills.
Both synthesis skills enforce identical minimum-3-instance threshold before proceeding. Both offer --min-N override.

website-synthesis source tier weighting (T1-T4, 6:1 ratio) applies throughout convergence scoring, divergence priority, theme evidence strength, and candidate ranking. repo-synthesis treats all repos as equal evidence sources.
website-synthesis convergence formula verifies independence: sites that cite each other count as 1 source, not 2. repo-synthesis has no independence verification.
repo-analysis REFERENCE.md phase numbering (Section 15 and agent allocation table) reflects a pre-v4.0 schema that no longer matches current SKILL.md phase numbers
